title: pittosporum truncatum
intro: Pittosporum truncatum, also known as the Tāwī, is a small tree native to New Zealand and eastern Australia, characterized by its dark green leaves and clusters of small, fragrant flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Pittosporum truncatum include relief from respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
